name: Formal Verification & Theorem Proving
def: Mathematically proving a system satisfies a formal specification.
problem: Guarantee the absence of whole bug classes where testing can only show presence.
mechanism: Express the spec and system in a logic; a proof assistant (Coq, Isabelle, Lean) or automated prover establishes the implementation refines/satisfies the spec.
benefits: Proof, not just evidence, of correctness
limits: Spec must be right (and match reality)
Scaling to large systems is hard
failures: Proving the model, not the deployed code
myths: "Verified = bug-free" — only w.r.t. the spec and its assumptions
